description.task: Define a Car class with three fields (make, model, year), a constructor, and a displayInfo() method. Create two Car objects in main().
instructions: Declare three fields: String make, String model, int year.
Write a constructor that accepts all three values and assigns them to the fields.
Write a displayInfo() method that prints: 'Make: Toyota | Model: Corolla | Year: 2022'.
In main(), create two Car objects: Car1 (Toyota, Corolla, 2022) and Car2 (Honda, Civic, 2021), call displayInfo() on both.
description.hint: Use 'this.make = make' in the constructor to assign parameter values to instance fields when they share the same name.
description.expectedOutput:
Make: Toyota | Model: Corolla | Year: 2022
Make: Honda | Model: Civic | Year: 2021

description.task: Create a Student class with both a default constructor and a parameterized constructor. Demonstrate both constructors in main().
description.context: A default constructor takes no arguments and sets default values. A parameterized constructor allows custom values at object creation time.
instructions: Write a default constructor (no parameters) that sets name = "Unknown" and age = 0.
Write a parameterized constructor that accepts String name and int age.
Do NOT change display() or main().
description.hint: When you define any constructor, Java no longer provides the default one automatically — you must define both explicitly.
description.expectedOutput:
Name: Unknown, Age: 0
Name: Alice, Age: 20

description.task: Write a Counter class that tracks a count value. Create two independent Counter objects and show that they each maintain their own state.
description.context: Each object in Java has its own copy of instance fields. Changes to one object's state do not affect another object of the same class.
instructions: Implement the constructor, increment(), and display() methods.
increment() must add 1 to the instance field 'count'.
description.hint: Each 'new Counter()' call creates a fresh object with its own count field — c1 and c2 are completely independent.
